context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct network-specific chargeback ratio thresholds, applies the same calendar-month calculation window as Visa, sends deadline reminders at the right intervals, flags repeat disputers with the correct lookback period and count, and recommends checkout-level friendly fraud prevention and reason-code-segmented win-rate analytics.

checklist.name: Visa warning threshold 0.65%
checklist.description: monitoring.js uses 0.0065 (0.65%) as the Visa early-warning threshold — NOT 1% or any other value

checklist.name: Visa critical threshold 1.00%
checklist.description: monitoring.js uses 0.0100 (1.00%) as the Visa critical/monitoring-program threshold

checklist.name: Mastercard warning threshold 1.00%
checklist.description: monitoring.js uses 0.0100 (1.00%) as the Mastercard early-warning threshold

checklist.name: Mastercard critical threshold 1.50%
checklist.description: monitoring.js uses 0.0150 (1.50%) as the Mastercard critical/excessive-chargeback threshold

checklist.name: Calendar-month ratio window
checklist.description: Chargeback ratio is computed as chargebacks in a calendar month divided by transactions in the same calendar month (not rolling 30 days or other window)

checklist.name: Deadline reminder at T-5 days
checklist.description: deadline-alerts.js sends a reminder 5 days before the response_due_date for open disputes

checklist.name: Deadline reminder at T-2 days
checklist.description: deadline-alerts.js sends a second reminder 2 days before the response_due_date

checklist.name: Repeat disputer: 2+ in 12 months
checklist.description: repeat-disputer.js flags a customer when they have 2 or more chargebacks within the past 12 months — not 3+, not a different time window

checklist.name: Checkout refund policy checkbox
checklist.description: design-doc.md recommends adding an explicit refund policy acceptance checkbox (with timestamp) at checkout to combat friendly fraud

checklist.name: Win-rate by reason code
checklist.description: design-doc.md describes segmenting win-rate analytics by reason code (e.g. 10.4 vs 13.1, or 'item not received' vs 'not as described') rather than reporting a single aggregate win rate

checklist.name: Respond to all disputes
checklist.description: design-doc.md or code notes that disputes should be responded to regardless of amount, since accepting chargebacks still counts against the ratio

checklist.name: Escalation path awareness
checklist.description: design-doc.md describes the dispute escalation path (dispute → representment → pre-arbitration → arbitration) and notes that each step has fees, indicating when to concede
